headlines.title: Unemployment rate unexpectedly falls as fewer students look for work
headlines.description: The drop has been largely driven by a rise in the number of people not actively seeking work.

headlines.title: Petrol thefts surge as Iran war pushes up fuel costs
headlines.description: One petrol retailer says he is experiencing about five drive-offs a week at each forecourt, costing him thousands.

headlines.title: Boy, 17, charged with Kenton synagogue arson
headlines.description: A bottle containing a type of accelerant was thrown through the window of the synagogue.

headlines.title: Apple names new boss to replace Tim Cook after 15 years
headlines.description: Ternus will take over running the technology giant in September as Cook steps up to become executive chairman.

headlines.title: King says late Queen may have been 'troubled deeply' by world we live in
headlines.description: The final design for a statue of the late Queen Elizabeth is to be unveiled, 100 years after her birth.

headlines.title: US singer D4vd pleads not guilty to murder in death of missing teen girl
headlines.description: Prosecutors say the singer, real name David Anthony Burke, could face life in prison due to the nature of the case, if convicted.

headlines.title: High electricity bills targeted in planned shakeup to energy pricing
headlines.description: The war in the Middle East has brought renewed attention to Britain's vulnerability to energy price shocks.

headlines.title: Phones to be banned in schools by law in England under government plans
headlines.description: Education minister Jacqui Smith said the move would create "a clear legal requirement for schools".

headlines.title: If Plaid win in Wales, that won't mean independence - at least not yet
headlines.description: In the Senedd campaign, are weighing up whether the issue of Welsh independence is a vote winner or loser.

headlines.title: The 20-somethings juggling three jobs to make ends meet
headlines.description: While the UK unemployment rate stands at 4.9%, increasing numbers of those in work have more than one job.

headlines.title: Youngest transfusion patient 'now chirpy little boy' after treatment in the womb
headlines.description: A rare blood transfusion while in the womb saved a baby's life when his mum was 16 weeks pregnant.

headlines.title: What we know about the Iranian ship seized by the US
headlines.description: The US has intercepted an Iranian ship entering the Gulf as part of its naval blockade, US President Donald Trump has said.

headlines.title: Indian politicians are campaigning while holding fish. What is going on?
headlines.description: In West Bengal polls, fish moves from plate to politics, symbolising identity and cultural pride.

headlines.title: 'Dumb machine' promises a clean energy breakthrough
headlines.description: A stellarator is difficult to build, but could it be the best way to make fusion energy work?

headlines.title: 'Why is the NHS funding students if it can't give them jobs?' Anger over recruitment freeze
headlines.description: Dozens of midwifery, physiotherapy and nursing students contact BBC over fears they won't get jobs.

headlines.title: Funding cuts turning beautiful Loch Lomond into 'rubbish dump'
headlines.description: Lost funding means a community group is no longer able to empty bins on the Loch Lomond tourist route.

headlines.title: Japan loosens arms export rules in break from post-WW2 pacifism
headlines.description: Eight decades of pacifism looks to be dismantling since Sanae Takaichi became Prime Minister in October 2025.

headlines.title: Two children die in stabbings each month, study finds
headlines.description: The average age of fatal knife attacks against children is 14 years old, a new study has revealed.

headlines.title: James Bond and prog rock to feature in 2026 Proms
headlines.description: Concerts celebrating Benjamin Britten, Steve Reich and Miles Davis also appear in the programme.

headlines.title: WW2 veteran who served in Navy despite seasickness dies aged 109
headlines.description: Frank Chester is praised by his family following his death nine days after his birthday.

headlines.title: Canadian killed in shooting at Mexico's ancient Teotihuacán pyramids
headlines.description: Several people were also injured in the incident on Monday at the popular tourist site, say Mexican officials, weeks before the country hosts the Fifa World Cup.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: MPs Grill Starmer Over Mandelson (Again)
headlines.description: PM defends his actions over the appointment of Mandelson.

headlines.title: Americanswers… on 5 Live! Is insider trading corrupting Trump's White House?
headlines.description: And will new claims about FBI Director Kash Patel’s behaviour get him fired?

headlines.title: Spurs lifeline or good step for West Ham - who's favourite for drop?
headlines.description: With five games remaining, BBC Sport looks at which side is most likely to take the third relegation spot.

headlines.title: Bolt advises Gout not to forget track and field
headlines.description: Usain Bolt urges Australian sprinter Gout Gout not to forget track and field and find a good support network to keep him focused on his career.

headlines.title: Ranking the most successful world record transfers of the past 50 years
headlines.description: Stumping up world record sums doesn't always guarantee success, but plenty of football's most expensive players have delivered on their hefty fees -  here are the top 10.

headlines.title: Why Wolves' relegation had been coming after slow decline
headlines.description: Wolves have been relegated from the Premier League after just three wins in their first 33 games of the season.

headlines.title: What to do about Rew? England's dilemma
headlines.description: James Rew's start to the season makes him a strong contender for an England call-up, but how would he fit into the Test team?

headlines.title: Red Bull's difficult start and 1976 cars compared with 2026 - F1 Q&A
headlines.description: BBC Sport F1 correspondent Andrew Benson answers your latest questions.
